name: Murus Gallicus
author: Phil Leduc (2009) / AGP port
description: Phil Leduc's 2009 breakthrough game on an 8x7 board, named for Caesar's Gallic siege walls. Each side starts with eight two-stone towers on its home row. A tower moves by distributing its two stones onto the next two cells in a straight line (each must be empty or a friendly wall); single stones are immobile walls. A tower may sacrifice a stone to demolish an adjacent enemy wall. Reach the opponent's home row, or leave them without a move, to win.
